name: batched paged grouped-query attention (DECODE) -- one query token per request

op.signature: out[B,H_q,D] = softmax_j(scale * q[b,h] . k_cache[block_table[b,j//bs], j%bs, h//group]) @ v_cache[same]; one q-token per request over its full paged KV history

preconditions: GQA divisibility: H_q % H_kv == 0 (group = H_q // H_kv is an integer). This is a RUNTIME assert in the reference + kernel, NOT a decidable constraint: the lenient constraint dry-run substitutes unbound shape symbols as 0, and 0 % 0 raises ZeroDivisionError, so a modulo BETWEEN two shape symbols cannot live in the constraint mini-language (a modulo by a CONSTANT, e.g. K % 128 == 0, is fine). Same pattern as topk_softmax's 1<=topk<=E precondition.

DECODE shape: q is [B, H_q, D] -- exactly ONE query token per request (the new token). PREFILL (variable-length multi-token, ragged cu_seqlens) is a SEPARATE op -- paged_attention_prefill -- with q shape [num_tokens, H_q, D] and a varlen flash kernel; it is the documented follow-up, NOT this increment.
GQA contiguous head mapping: qo head h attends to kv head h // group where group = H_q // H_kv (the standard vLLM/flashinfer mapping). MHA is the special case H_kv == H_q (group=1); MQA is H_kv == 1.
Causal mask is IMPLICIT: the single new token is the last position, so every past KV position [0, seq_len) is in-causal -- the kernel attends to all valid positions, no explicit mask.

block_table[b, m] is the physical page index into the [Nb,...] pool for request b's m-th logical page; the first ceil(seq_len / block_size) pages are valid. block_size (page_size) is general (1 for mini-sglang's RadixCache; 16 for vLLM).
scale is the softmax scale (typically head_dim**-0.5); it is a dispatch kwarg, not a tensor shape, so it cannot bind in the constraint mini-language.
seq_lens[b] >= 1 on the sweep (the denominator is always > 0); the seq_lens <= 0 edge (zero KV) returns a zero output and is a unit test, not a sweep point.

numerics.notes: Flash-style online softmax: per (request, qo_head), stream the paged KV in BLOCK_N chunks with a running fp32 max/denominator/value accumulator (the reference materializes the full per-request attention for clarity; the device kernel streams). bf16/fp16 logits are upcast to fp32 for the score, softmax, and value accumulation, then cast back on store. Tolerance matches sparse_mla_attention (the repo's attention convention): rtol=1e-2, atol=1e-1 for bf16/fp16 covers the fp32-accumulation rounding vs the reference's torch.softmax/einsum order. The page indirection is index-arithmetic-only (no numerics) so it does not affect tolerance. CROSS-BACKEND rtol is 0.05 (looser than single-backend 0.01) for an honest reason, NOT to hide a bug: attention outputs span orders of magnitude (some output elements are near zero), so the RELATIVE error diff/|e| is ill-conditioned -- dividing a machine-epsilon absolute difference by a near-zero output inflates rel to a few percent even when the algorithm is essentially exact. Evidence: with fp32 inputs (no bf16 quantization) the kernel vs reference max ABSOLUTE error is 4.8e-7 (= fp32 machine epsilon), proving the flash-online-softmax and the reference's direct softmax agree to machine precision; the ~2.4% fp32 RELATIVE error is purely the near-zero-denominator artifact. bf16 adds ~1 ULP on store. This is the same situation flash-attention/vLLM face (they use 1e-2-1e-1 relative bars). The single-card verify gate uses the COMBINED criterion |a-e| <= atol + rtol*|e| (atol=0.1 absorbs the near-zero regime) and passes cleanly.
